description: Concert tour booking platform for music beginners — find tribute shows, beginner-friendly classical matinees, and small-venue gigs, with a chronological 'your tour itinerary' planner

pages.name: home
pages.description: Landing page that explains the service and invites first-time concertgoers to start planning
components: Top navigation with logo, primary links (Discover, Itineraries, Beginner's guide, Venues), a 'Sign in' link, and a 'Start planning' button

Hero panel with eyebrow text 'Your first concert, sorted', headline, supporting paragraph, and a search panel containing a city input, a date-range picker, a genre dropdown ('I don't know yet' as default), and a 'Find shows' primary button
Three-step explainer row titled 'How it works' — each step has a numbered marker, short title (Pick a city, Build your night, Show up), and one-line description
Curated 'Easy first concerts' shelf — a row of five show cards, each with cover image, artist name, venue name, date chip, ticket price, and a 'beginner-friendly' tag
Footer with four columns (Explore, Help, Company, Legal), a small newsletter input, and a copyright line

animations.element: Search panel (city, date, genre, button) inside the hero block

pages.name: discover
pages.description: Browsable list of upcoming shows with filters and a map preview
components: Same top navigation
Page header with title 'Shows near you', a result-count subtitle, and a view-toggle (List / Map) on the right

Filter rail across the top — chips for Date (Tonight, This weekend, Next 30 days, Custom), a Genre dropdown, a Price range slider, a Venue-size selector (Intimate, Mid, Arena), and a 'Beginner-friendly only' toggle

Main results grid (three per row) — each show card has cover art, artist name, support acts line, venue + city, date and door-time, price-from, capacity icon, and a small 'Add to itinerary' plus button
Right-side static map preview card listing five numbered pins with venue name, distance, and the show headlining there tonight

animations.element: View-toggle (List / Map) control on the right side of the page header

pages.name: show
pages.description: Single show detail page with ticket tiers and a beginner orientation
components: Same top navigation

Show hero with cover image on the left and a panel on the right showing artist name, support acts, venue + city, date and door-time, age policy, and two buttons ('Get tickets' primary, 'Add to itinerary' secondary)
Ticket-tier table — rows for General admission, Seated balcony, and Early-entry, each with description, price, remaining-availability bar, and a select button
'What to expect' beginner panel — four labelled rows: Show length, Volume level (a 1–5 dot scale), Standing vs seated, Crowd vibe, each with a one-sentence explainer
Setlist preview accordion listing typical songs from recent tour dates, plus a venue info block with address, transit lines, nearest food, and a small static map thumbnail

animations.element: Cover image on the left side of the show hero

pages.name: itinerary
pages.description: Chronological timeline of the user's planned concerts across a trip
components: Same top navigation
Itinerary header with editable trip name, date range, destination city, total-shows count, total-spend figure, and a 'Share itinerary' button

Vertical timeline as the main content — each day is a dated row with a day label on the left rail and event cards on the right showing time, show title, venue, walking distance, ticket status (Booked, Reserved, Wishlist), and a remove control
Gap rows between timeline events labelled 'free time' with three nearby suggestions (food spot, late-night venue, record store) each as a tappable mini-card
Right-hand summary sidebar with a packing-style checklist (tickets, ID, earplugs, cash for merch), a budget breakdown bar by category (Tickets, Travel, Food, Extras), and a 'Download .ics' button

animations.element: Editable trip-name field and date range inside the itinerary header

pages.name: guide
pages.description: Beginner's guide explaining genres, venue types, and etiquette
components: Same top navigation
Guide header with title 'A beginner's guide to live music', a one-paragraph intro, and a table-of-contents chip row (Genres, Venues, Etiquette, Glossary)
Genres section — a grid of six genre cards, each with genre name, a one-line description, three example artists, a typical-volume dot scale, and an 'Easy entry point' suggested show link
Venue-types comparison panel — three side-by-side columns (Intimate club, Theatre, Arena) listing capacity range, typical ticket price, dress code, sightline notes, and 'good for beginners?' verdict
Etiquette FAQ accordion with eight items (When to clap, Phones up or down, Mosh pits, Talking during songs, Encores, Merch lines, Photography, Leaving early)

animations.element: Table-of-contents chip row inside the guide header
